title: CustomNodeRegistry — a node-kind extensibility standard: bounded delimiter-grammar registration that materializes into DOM node subclasses (customNodes : node kinds :: customElements : tags)
summary: Prep research for decision #2074 (surfaced from the #1989 residue-grammar session 2026-07-01, which it now blocks; we:reports/2026-07-01-custom-node-registry.md). The question: is a node-KIND extensibility standard worth proposing — a bounded, safe way to register new delimiter-based syntaxes that materialize into DOM nodes — and in what shape? CODE GROUND TRUTH (frontierui): the 'register a node kind + upgrade-walk' pattern ALREADY ships as FOUR parallel plug families — webexpressions Text parsers (CustomTextNodeParserRegistry -> CustomTextNode; base class carries a 2-char openingIdentifier/closingIdentifier grammar at fui:plugs/webexpressions/CustomTextNodeParser.ts:39,45, with shipped {{/}} DoubleCurlyBracketParser and [[/]] DoubleSquareBracketParser), webdirectives Comment (CustomCommentRegistry -> CustomComment), template (CustomTemplateTypeRegistry -> CustomTemplateType), and script (CustomScriptTypeRegistry -> CustomScriptType). #1986 codified the three directive registries as SIBLING registries (we:docs/agent/block-standard.md:568-590); platform-decisions already says 'reuse its grammar' for {{ }}/[[ ]] (we:docs/agent/platform-decisions.md:747,1018-1019). No CustomNodeRegistry exists — it would be the generalization. PRIOR ART, standards-track: (1) DOM Parts / Template Instantiation (WICG) is the closest work — {{ }} syntax INSIDE the inert <template> carrier (no top-level parser change), modelling the new thing as HANDLES into existing nodes and DELIBERATELY AVOIDING a new Node subclass; stalled on scope/perf/vendor-consensus (2025-03-26 W3C minutes). (2) Custom Elements HYPHEN RULE is the canonical extensibility-bounding technique: a name must contain a hyphen 'for namespacing and forward compatibility' — the platform forfeits a naming subspace so userland never collides with future built-ins. (3) The DOM node-kind set is CLOSED (Node.nodeType is a small closed integer enum) and there is NO prior art for a new userland nodeType/Node subclass — every mechanism extends WITHIN Element or adds HANDLES onto existing nodes. This is the load-bearing RISK in 'node-KIND' framing. (4) FRESH on-point precedent: WHATWG PR #12118 (merged 2026-06-25) makes HTML parse <?target data?> into a real ProcessingInstruction node, gated by a target-name grammar + xml/xml-stylesheet blocklist — the platform actively re-opening a delimiter->non-Element-node path with exactly the grammar+blocklist bounding technique this proposal wants. (5) Inert-carrier / opt-in precedents: <template> inert content model, <script type=importmap|...> data blocks, <!DOCTYPE html> mode switch, 'use strict' directive prologue — cross-cutting law: successful extensions hide new syntax in an already-inert carrier OR reuse a token legacy parsers tolerate as a no-op. (6) Scoped Custom Element Registries (WHATWG #10854) precedent for a scoped/pluggable registry shape. PRIOR ART, framework delimiter grammars: {{ dominates the interpolation opener (7/10 engines); 2-char openers are modal; per-character REFLECTED close is well-precedented (Liquid/Jinja {%/%}, {#/#}; Angular banana [(/)]; Razor @*/*@) — the proposed {$/$} is structurally identical to {%/%}; the lone counter-case is ERB <%/%> (tag-echo, not mirror). The expression-vs-statement TWO-FAMILY split ({{ }} emit-value vs {% %} control-flow) is NEAR-UNIVERSAL, so keying node kinds by opening delimiter is aligned with prior art. Escaping is universal+mandatory (every {{-engine ships a raw/verbatim hatch + delimiter-override because {{ collides when template layers stack). SYNTHESIS -> forks: Fork 1 (what is a custom node kind) forced toward MATERIALIZE-INTO-EXISTING-KIND-SUBCLASS (Text/Comment/Element), NOT a new nodeType (zero prior art) — the literal new-kind is the documented north-star residual; Fork 2 (unification scope) one shared CustomNodeKind registration CONTRACT that per-carrier registries implement (predicate/interface unity, mirroring #1989 Fork 2 and #1986's sibling structure) vs one monolithic registry (dominated); Fork 3 (delimiter grammar) 2-char opening + per-char reflected close + expression/statement two-family split + MANDATORY escape hatch/override, vs allowing non-reflected tag-echo closes (rejected). Supported-by-default: extends-Element-or-not is per-kind config (the carrier decides), expression interpolation is already one instance (webexpressions). Generalizes #1986 — its three registries become instances of the node-kind contract; reconcile at codification, don't duplicate.
